compressed_prompt: write song lyrics on socio-economic issue; english, all lowercase; no commas
response:
# **empty pockets full city**

the landlord raised the rent again
on the first of every month
we work our hands down to the bone
and still come up with nothing

they built a tower on our block
where the corner store once stood
they call it progress call it growth
we call it leaving for good

*chorus:*
we are the ones who build the walls
but never get to live inside
we are the ones who cook the meals
but go to bed hungry at night
the numbers on the screen go up
the numbers in our pockets fall
they say the tide will lift all boats
but some of us don't have boats at all

the kids are sharing textbooks now
the roof is leaking through
the politician shakes his hands
and says he feels it too

minimum wage and maximum hours
still short on the electric bill
they say just work a little harder
like we haven't always will

*chorus*

*bridge:*
it ain't about being lazy
it ain't about a lack of drive
it's about who owns the table
and who just tries to survive

*outro:*
the city shines so bright at night
on the side we never see
one world for those who hold the keys
and one world left for you and me
